reviewDescription:
In May we needed an RV dropped at the NASCAR races in Ft Worth. We originally went to RVShare. We were cancelled on 4 times - the last one was 1 week before the race!!!  We had already bought our tickets. We had already paid for our Parking Spot our camping spot. I was furious!!  So then we went to Outdoorsy - 2 people cancelled on us and then We got one that he said I’m so sorry y’all went through all this, don’t worry I will not be canceling on you. I got you. I’ve never dropped an RV at the Texas Motor Speedway, but don’t worry I got it. Well on Sunday night he was going through the rules to bring the RV in and I don’t know what happened but he said he just couldn’t do it. And he canceled on us!!!  We had four days before we were leaving for the races. 

So then I found Triple w Rentals. Jaden had called me when we had already gotten the guy at Outdoorsy so I told him we didn’t need him. He said if you do, here’s my cell phone number. Call me. Well lo and behold The guy canceled on us so I called him back. Jaden called me back right away. He could not have been nicer. He was so. understanding of what we went through and how stressed I was. He was doing everything he could to let us know that we had nothing to worry about. They had an RV left that they could bring to the track. It wasn’t the best one they had but it fit our four grandsons, my husband and I. They got there on Friday morning. Got everything set up. They were so kind. Randy was so nice and did not get bothered at all with my many many texts back-and-forth. 

Every time I got a call or a text from them, my heart just sank I was immediately thinking they’re gonna cancel on this too. But they did not - they were so extremely helpful so extremely accommodating. 

Whenever we need an RV again, we are definitely coming back to Triple W. They will go anywhere and bring an RV to wherever you need it to be. If there’s any problems at all, they are on it. They will get it fixed. Things happen they just do, but they were just so kind and accommodating. I cannot stress enough how wonderful this company is. With the other two places you are dependent on independent owners of these RV’s and they can back out in a second as we found out over and over and over again.  And we get charged for booking with them!    Yes they charged as for the privilege of being canceled on.😳. 

But with Triple W they own so many RVs. They have plenty for everyone and you are not dependent on a personal owner. They have some beautiful, beautiful campers and RVs. Tell them Miss Lou sent you. ❤️🙏🏼🦋

reviewTitle: Inferior parts, unmaintained, unsafe RV suspension (snapped leaf spring); ZERO financial recovery for parts and labor
reviewDescription:
I rented an RV from an owner in IL who placed his previously damaged RV into RVShare inventory.  One of his leaf springs (single axle) (4 layers thick) previously failed and was replaced with a more robust 3 layer spring (of which the owner denied, "that he bought the 2022 RV brand new).  Upon rental, the other, original spring failed on me, the 4th and longest spring snapped, shredding the tire in the wheel well.  I immediately called Customer Service because I was in a remote area, on the Emergency shoulder of the road, just inside the main gate of a military compound.  Since the base is on a higher threat level of protection they supplied the approved towing company and mechanics who are cleared to tow me to my designated spot and make repairs once parts are secured (new pair of matching springs and tires); to keep all receipts (parts and labor) for reimbursement.  I did so.  All parts were purchased, trained mechanics replaced both springs, mounted, balanced and torqued both tires along w/ removing all "old" grease (lost its viscosity) with fresh grease.  The best tire was used to replace the older spare.

Upon inspection, it was noticed that the failed spring was missing the whole 3rd layer on one side so there was insufficient support for the 4th layer, hence the failure.  The proof was provided in close up imagery.

After several days I further noticed water on the floor, that there was a leaky kitchen hot water line, mold everywhere under the cabinet.  I purchased plumbers teflon tape and made the correct repairs (it was never sealed properly, no tape previously existed).  I was forced to make another repair and clean up the mold so I wouldn't get sick.  Even though I was apparently breathing this for over a week.  All photos were provided and Customer Service contacted.

All receipts, all photos (35ish) were supplied to RVShare along with up to date calls to their Customer Service.  Always stating at the end of conversation, we're here to help...

After returning the RV (cleaned, systems flushed) and accepted by the owner)), I filed my request for reimbursement and was Denied because I signed a Departure Form.  "Everything appeared" to be fine, as if it's reasonable for a renter to crawl under the RV and approve the quality of the parts...  RVShare stated they have zero responsibility in this matter.

I was further informed that I can file for labor expenses, but not parts.  I denied this "offer" because it was less than the total, but primarily because, why am I responsible for maintaining and paying for repairs for something I don't even own?  All for the privilege of renting from RVShare?

Then told that I can open a Dispute (a long and time-consuming process) only to be Denied again, stating that all expenses I incurred are "outside the platform" and I need to seek remedy from the owner.  

I did so, and he states that this should be covered by RVShare or under the insurance policy I paid for.  All of the burden placed on me to have remedy.  Both avenues I requested restitution from and was further Denied, RVShare has no responsibility, seek remedy from the owner.

I shared with RVShare that I think their platform was hijacked and hoodwinked by this owner by them knowingly placing their unsafe and negligently maintained RV into their inventory, jeopardizing their reputation.  Apparently they don't care because they don't stand behind the product they promote.  This should make everyone question what their (RVShares) Participant RV Owners Terms and Conditions Contract states regarding providing a safe RV into their inventory in the first place.

Bottom Line: DO NOT RENT THROUGH RVSHARE.  If you have a problem, they simply say, you signed the Departure Form, accepted the RV "as is".  As if we as renters once again are crawling under the RV to check for an unsafe, inferior suspension system, leaky, moldy faucets, etc.  btw; water doesn't accumulate unless the sink is turned on for a a period of time.  Which is not done when you "test" whether you have running water when you pick up the RV on day one.

This is a RENTER BEWARE Review.  Many owners place their RV's into RVShare inventory to offset their monthly offsite storage fees without paying the necessary costs of continued maintenance.

RVShare does not share ANY responsibility and hides behind legal jargon for their protection, instead of doing what's right.  Their temporary "Insurance" is also apparently worthless.

The only way this will improve is through a loss in rental revenue because there's a boycott; we simply go elsewhere.  Talk is cheap, "we're here for you, we understand and sympathize with your situation, blah, blah, hiding behind some clause within a "Departure Form".  Since it's their Form, the T&C's (Terms & Conditions) are always in favor of the "entity" that provides it...

reviewTitle: The experience with the rv owner was…
reviewDescription:
The experience with the rv owner was great but rv share insurance is terrible.

reviewTitle: This might be the biggest scam going…
reviewDescription: This might be the biggest scam going today. Not only did this place cancel our reservation 10 days before our trip. They also screwed us on the insurance and are now randomly charging our credit card that I cannot get off my trip Rental agreement. Not only do they not listen they are condescending and ridiculous on the phone. The owner has been awesome and he’s complaining about them as well and saying how shady they actually are. I have attempt to contact rv share to ask for help, but the customer service chat goes blank after waiting for 20 or 30 minutes to be connect to a live agent. The phone number is impossible. Not sure if I got scammed at this point.

reviewDescription: Beware this company! We rented an RV from them and were very upfront about 1 minor scratch that occurred on the first day my husband was driving the RV. We were expecting to lose our $1500 security deposit but were FLOORED when they proceeded to charge our card for double that amount to cover "repairs."  The company refused to share any repair estimate with us and charged our card without our authorization. They are not upfront in their policies and do not care about their renters.

We’d like to look into this further for you. Please reach out to us directly or respond to any existing claim communication so our team can review the details with you.

reviewTitle: I booked an rv on the web site and it got canceled by rv share with no reason.
reviewDescription:
I booked an rv on the web site. Communicated with steven the owner. I liked the trailer and so i submitted a booking request. Steven quickly accepted. Went to pay and during the process i chose the insurance package that worked for us. 
Clicked pay and everything was good until a email came back stating rv share needed more information. Then minutes later another email came through stating reservation was canceled. So reason just canceled.
Called customer service and they knew nothing as to why reservation was canceled.  Blamed it on the insurance company. 
I have a clean drivers record.  No felony or misdemeanor convictions ever. Only court record is of a divorce in 1989. 
I went to log back into my account to relay that the rental was canceled but rv share blocked my account. Not sure why. So i called back to cutomer service. They said they were told to block my account. Lol. Asked why and the person said he did not know why. 
I asked for a supervor manager to call me on monday. Hey said he woukd put in the request. I doubt they will call. This company seems disorganized and not cutomer friendly. 
I reserved a 5th wheel from Outdoorsy and everything went smooth as silk. Makes me wonder why Outdoorsy can rent me an RV but rv share cant. Lol.
My recommendation is to not rent from rv share.

reviewTitle: RVshare does not care for the RV owners!
reviewDescription:
Ive been renting out my van on RVshare for a year now. I just listed a new vehicle, a RV, this week and the insurance got approved as of Monday. Last night on Friday I got a reservation request for that new vehicle for the day of. I talked to the renter and approved the reservation, not knowing the RV was blocked from insurance coverage due to needing a document signed that I have never seen. RVshare never emailed me or warned me of this missing document before approving the reservation. I called RVshare immediately for assistance and I could not get the form signed in time for the reservation so I had to cancel the reservation that I made just 15 minutes before and they forced me to pay $165 to cancel the reservation that shouldn’t of been available in the first place. RVshare obviously does not care for the RV owners that use their platform and make them money. I will no longer be renting out my vehicles on RVshare from now on. 

The next Monday, I did receive an email from RVshare retracting the cancellation fee so I do appreciate that. I hope they can prevent this from happening to anyone else in the future.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hello Kara, we’re sorry for the frustration this caused. This isn’t the experience we want for our owners. To activate coverage, we do need certain documents (like registration and/or a signed consignment form), and it looks like these were still pending at the time of booking. That said, we understand how this led to a difficult situation with a same-day reservation. Our team has reached out. Please follow up via email so we can finalize this with you. We appreciate your feedback and your time hosting on RVshare.

reviewTitle: $375 prep fee for a dirty RV and no support from the company
reviewDescription: We were charged a $375 prep fee by the owner. Upon pickup, it was impossible to see how disgusting the RV really was. Urine around the base of the toilet. Something nasty on the shower floor (under a rug at pickup). Fridge and pans with deep dirt and/or mold. Because the individual owner was talking so poorly of other renters, we didn't want to raise concerns until after the trip. We spent hours cleaning before we could even load things. I talked to a person at customer service, and she seemed disgusted. She said it would be wise to wait until the return form had been fully completed. We did. We provided dated evidence of all the things, and we received nothing back stating we agreed to it at pick-up state. They offered us a discount code. I can honestly say we will never use this company again and will go with a company that doesn't have individual owners playing games.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hi, we're so sorry to hear about your experience. We’re happy to look into this issue for you. Please reach us at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

reviewTitle: We took a trip up to Oklahoma from…
reviewDescription: We took a trip up to Oklahoma from Texas to Cherokee Landing State Park at Lake Tenkiller. Not only was the park and the weather perfect for the weekend, but our RV was the icing on the cake! Our hosts, Haley and Chad have got this RV hosting thing figured out! Our RV was the most comfortable we've ever stayed in. Well maintained and immaculately clean, we didn't need Anything! They thought of Everything! We will definitely go thru them again! Booking was so easy, but the communication was Top Notch, we had questions....They had quick answers
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you, Randy! We love to hear this!

reviewDescription: Easy to work with. Delivered when promised.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you, Danielle!

reviewTitle: VERY DECEPTIVE and charges will be significantly more than what is shown at confirmation.
reviewDescription: RV Share is very deceptive and not up front about what the total cost amount is. Basically whatever the rental cost is, double it to equal the "required insurance" fees and service fees. This is not disclosed in the upfront amount but said in a very nondescript way at the bottom of the reservation confirmation email that unless this "required" insurance is paid, in a separate charge, the reservation will be canceled. These extra fees are charged whether you're having the RV delivered and parked (not driving it) or driving the wheels off of it. But wait, that's not all......when we decided to cancel because the amount it costs to rent a camper is the same as renting an entire brand new 4 bedroom lake house on the very same lake, you won't get a full refund even though we made the reservation 3 weeks ago for 6 MONTHS from now. They will keep the service fees for all your time, effort and frustration that they have caused you when they nearly double what you thought was the total cost was going to be. Thumbs down. Do not recommend. Way too many additional and required charges to sleep in a camper for a few nights. You can literally get a 3 bedroom ocean front condo on a beach or 4  bedroom lake house for what this website wants to sneak in as "required". Very disappointed. Would never recommend even to someone I don't like that much.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hello Diana, thanks for reaching out to us. We make every effort to be as transparent as possible with all users of our platform regarding any administrative or service fees they may be subject to. At the time of booking, there is a breakdown of the rental price, any owner’s fees, and any taxes or RVshare fees. If you have any questions regarding these fees, please contact our Customer Experience team at 1-888-482-0234 open 24/7.

reviewDescription: For the most part, everything went well with the rv rental.  The only issue was the height measurement of the toy-hauler loading door.  The website description/measurement was actually one inch higher than the actual door header height.  One inch may not seem like a lot but it does matter if you are loading an off road vehicle.  Friendly suggestion is to verify all measurements before paying a deposit, as I could not get a refund, even though the website measurement was incorrect.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hello Jake, we’re sorry about the discrepancy in the door height. While RV owners are responsible for listing details, we understand how important accurate measurements are, especially in situations like yours. We’ll share this feedback with our team to help improve accuracy moving forward.

reviewTitle: Sharing my great experience with Kyle. Ambasador of the RVShare brand.
reviewDescription:
I would like to share my great experience with Kyle with the RVShare leadership team. 

This is our first time renting a travel trailer and I had questions after I booked the reservation. Kyle did an outstanding job patiently walking me through the answers to all my questions and details of the program in a way that was easy for me to understand. 

Your decision to keep a direct phone number and ability to talk to a real person is a company differentiator. Keep that as part of your brand promise. 

I will most likely rent again from RVShare as a result of my positive experience with Kyle.  Hopefully when it's time we can work with Kyle again. Kyle is an ambassador of the RVShare brand. 

Thank you Kyle. 
Phill
reviewCompanyResponse: Awesome to hear, Phill! Thank you!

reviewDescription:
Trailer Experience. 4*
RV Share 1*

I am not happy with how RV share handled our claim for the damage. They get a 1 Star. The claim was put in and I requested an itemized list of what was damaged and how I was charged for it. I was told that they only provide that to the owner. A little frustrating to be paying money for something without know what and why. The owner was nice enough to share the information with me. The summary only gave a price for parts and for labor, no detailed list on how they got that amount. Not a good system for openness about the money I am charged. I will look for other option for renting an RV rather than using RV share.

The RV and communication was good. Had a trailer driver side blowout on the return trip and found that the lug nut wrench was too flimsy to change a tire. Noted that 5 months earlier a renter had the same issue on the other side, not sure about the tires. The owners did mention the possibility of using a drill but did not have one to use. Luckily a Good Samaritan stopped to assist, otherwise we would have been there for a while awaiting assistance from roadside assistance.

I was able to pull the trailer with my 4runner, which is what I wanted. A little crowded my family of 5, but that is what I get for wanting to pull with my vehicle. The outside fridge and the freezer worked great. The inside fridge was not cool enough and I should have adjusted the setting but didn't. The AC worked well.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hello Ken, we apologize for the experience you faced with the damage claim. We can certainly put you back in contact with your adjuster if needed. Please give us a call at 1-888-482-0234 for further review.

reviewDescription:
We were owners of an RV listed on RVshare. The first year was great, until a renter wrecked our RV and caused $18,000 in damage. Renters are required to purchase insurance through RVshare when they rent, which is supposed to give owners peace of mind. Our renters did purchase insurance, but it came with a $4,000 deductible. The renters then canceled their credit card and have been impossible to get ahold of.

RVshare told us it was up to us to collect the deductible from the renters, and they offered no help at all. We are now without our RV to rent for income or to use ourselves, and we are stuck dealing with the financial loss.

Based on our experience, we do not believe RVshare is a trustworthy business. Owners should be aware of the risks before listing their RV.

If you want, I can also rewrite it to sound more formal, more aggressive, or more like a short review.

Terrible policies.  Rented an RV for Easter weekend months in advance and was contacted the day before to let me know that the RV had a mechanical issue and could not be rented.  This isn’t RV Share’s fault but when they refunded me for the rental, they kept $25 for a non-refundable screening fee and refused to refund it.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hello Mary, we’re very sorry your trip was impacted, especially after planning so far in advance.

While the cancellation was due to a mechanical issue outside of our control, we understand your frustration with the screening fee. Please give us a call with your reservation details, and we’d be happy to take another look and see how we can help.

reviewTitle: RV Share Customer Service is a JOKE
reviewDescription: The guy we rented the RV from was terrific, but the company itself is a joke. We paid $1000 for insurance and roadside assistance, and ended up needing roadside assistance. We called and asked for a tow to be dispatched at 7 pm. We were obviously talking to a call service in another country. We ended up had paying an extra $135 for the tow because it would be over 100 miles. The next morning we called twice to inquire about the ETA of the tow,and both times were told 45 minutes. We finally got the name of the towing company and called them ourselves and found out they had never been dispatched. The tow arrived in 30 minutes, the guy was able to get us started again and on our way. I doubt very much we will ever get reimbursed for the extra money we paid out. We will never use RV share again.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hello Ron, we’re really sorry to hear about your experience—this is not the level of service we aim to provide, especially in a situation where you needed timely roadside assistance. We’re happy to look into this issue for you. Please reach us at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

reviewTitle: Do NOT list your camper or camper van on RVShare!!
reviewDescription:
RVShare as a van owner has proven to be a complete nightmare. 

I had my camper van listed for 8 months - with zero interest - while outdoorsy and RVezy gave me consistent listings. 

I finally had someone book through there and after my. An had been listed for 8 months with ZERO communication - it was after my van was booked that they sent me an email with a 7 day deadline to provide proof of a recall repair. 

They have no clue how anything works. First - they only sent an email (I get 309 emails a day). They have my number to text. 

Second - they gave me 7 days to provide proof of a repair. My van has been in the shop since DECEMBER 1st!! I’m still waiting on parts and approvals for the work. 

They can’t even tell me SPECIFICALLY the work they are requiring proof of. When I pushed on that - then they wanted the list of maintenance items I was having them work on. 

I’ve had them completely remove my RV from their site. 

I recommend everyone do the same. They are scammers. 

Oh yeah. They were hacked and I received dozens of emails of scammers. Took them for ever to plug the leak.  Then their customer service folks all deny it. 

Check out Reddit
reviewCompanyResponse: Hi Soph, we're so sorry to hear about your experience. We’re happy to look into this issue for you. Please reach us at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

reviewDescription: I rented a stationary rv.  The owner told me I would not have to pay the insurance fee.  He was wrong.  RVshare would not honor the owner's committment and I ended up cancelling and having to pay the $135.15 service fee anyway, since insurance through RVshare is MANDATORY under ANY circumstances.
reviewCompanyResponse: We want to make sure you are still protected even if you are not driving the unit. We require insurance for stationary rentals because accidents still can happen when the RV doesn't move and RVs are expensive assets. Our protection plans will cover the unit in case of theft, vandalism, fire, flood, lightning, explosions, earthquakes, storms, hail, acts of God, etc.

reviewDescription: Tried to reserve an RV through their website, but the process was misleading. After submitting my reservation, I was told it needed approval, then prompted to pay for additional insurance that wasn’t included in the original quote. By the time I called to ask about the unexpected charge, they said the reservation had been approved and it was “past 24 hours,” so canceling cost me a $65 fee—even though approval happened almost a full day after I booked, giving me no real chance to clarify anything. Canceled because I didn’t trust the hidden fees. Still fighting to get my $65 back.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hello Paul, thanks for reaching out to us. We make every effort to be as transparent as possible with all users of our platform regarding any administrative or service fees they may be subject to. At the time of booking, there is a breakdown of the rental price, any owner’s fees, and any taxes or RVshare fees. If you have any questions regarding these fees, please contact our Customer Experience team at 1-888-482-0234 open 24/7.

reviewDescription:
Joy was very nice to work with

reviewDescription:

reviewDescription:

We booked in Oct 25 for a Feb 2026 trip.   This was going to be our first RV experience, and was very much looking forward to.

4 weeks before our trip we got a message from the RV owner the camper was damaged and will be out of commission for our dates.   Super bummed but I get it, this is not where i have the issue.

RV share provided refunds.....but here is the issue.....they WILL NOT REFUND the screening fee.  

I completely understand if WE cancelled the reservation we wouldn't get it back.   But 'WE' didn't cancel the reservation.  If nothing else the OWNER should absorb the charge.   

We tried to reason w RV share but they won't budge.   So now I need to find a hotel, on top of the charge from RV share.

For $26 they lost a customer for life. So stupid.

reviewTitle: RVshare has been hacked several times
reviewDescription: RVshare has been hacked several times. They continue to deny that they have been hacked. We have had to change our bank account to make sure that it is secure. We have documentation from the renters of the RV that they also where hacked. Instead of working with us to resolve the issue they decided to kick us off of the site. So, if you make waves you will be kicked from their site. I would love to provide the exact date but they kicked me from the site because I requested follow-up and insurances that this will not happened again. It happened 3 times in 8 week time frame.

On December 26th, an RV was to be delivered to my home at 12 PM. At 12:50 we contacted the owner to see if he was lost. We received no response so called RV share which told us our reservation was canceled at 12:55 PM. Five minutes after we contacted the owner. They tried to tell us the rv was damaged but was a little coincidental that the RV was not reported damaged and trip was not cancelled until an hour after arrival time and after we tried to contact owner. They took our deposit and promised they would get us a new RV to our home that day. They have a worry, free guarantee which is absolutely a joke. They said they called around to find RVs that were available and sent us a list of four. They said they personally called each owner to ensure they were able to accommodate us. When we contacted the owners, they were not available. The only one available was one without heat. I contacted them at least 10 times that day to tell them about the urgency of the situation. Told me several times a manager will call me with no response and no call back. We are out our money for 7 tickets to Buffalo Bills football game, $350 for  an RV spot at the stadium, supplies and above all a Christmas gift for my kids that they were looking forward to. RV share is not reliable and customer service is terrible. A Christmas present that was supposed to be memorable turned into a nightmare . Please don’t use this company .

reviewDescription: I called RV share in regards to tolls not being paid by a past renter. reviewDescription: I would NOT recommend this company if you are a citizen of any country that is not US. -- My wife and I are Canadians. We made many non refundable reservations at campgrounds & attractions in Colorado. 3 days before our final payment payment was due, RVshare told us that we required a US drivers license. We tried to explain that as Canadian citizens, it was impossible to get approved for a US drivers license,, to no avail. We lost several hundred dollars for these non-refundable reservations. reviewTitle: Worst rental experience of my life
reviewDescription: Worst rental experience of my life and I have rented 30 plus times.They bait and switch you. They get all your data, then start telling you there are more expenses and fees, more documents to fill out, more pictures to send. We spent almost 30 hours trying to get it finalized. They already had are credit card and personal data. The cancellation fees are almost the entire cost, so I kept trying to give them what they wanted. You can't use a computer or android phone to send them data. After almost 10 phone calls including 2 to a supervisor and about 20 emails and texts. They then wanted more photos saying are faces weren't clear. These are full facials and ID's sent to Asia, but they refuse to say where. Kept doing it, but it wasn't worth it. Granted the couple who owned the RV were great, the RV was not clean and the stove and awning didn't work. Would never recommend anyone use RVshare. And we are in the US. Their HQ's is an office in a strip mall in Akton OH. Not a real US company and who know what they do with your data. I got a new credit card after the trip.

reviewTitle: Broken RV, no compensation for it
reviewDescription: Basically, someone can rent you a broken RV and you get fully charged for it with no recourse other than to sue the owner directly. This platform does not protect its renters, only the RV owners. First year I used this had a positive experience. 2nd year I used it the owner rented us an RV that had a multitude of broken parts, due to one of which we were not able to use the water for two days. When we disputed everything including loss of use RV share said their terms don't allow them to reimburse for this. RV Share got fees for the RV, the owner made their money, we got screwed.

Kyle really represented your company extremely well. They showed concern were not in a rush, very helpful, and I was very anxious as an rv. Rental, I had since June and the trip was in about a month. Canceled on me when I told them the truth that there would be two people staying instead of one.
Kyle show concern and walked me through everything.You are very lucky to have somebody like that representing your company

reviewTitle: I rented a van through RVshare (DeVan…
reviewDescription:
I rented a van through RVshare (DeVan Rentals) for a long-planned camping trip near Beaver, Utah—an area close to 10,000 feet in elevation. What I wasn’t told until after picking up the van was that it was a retired Amazon delivery van, which absolutely matters when you’re headed into high mountain terrain. 
The van didn’t even start when I went to pick it up. The owner was out of town and never met me in person. Instead, he filled out the check-in form on my behalf before I even arrived, and had to call roadside assistance just to get the van running. That should have been the first red flag.
I drove the van home and, less than 30 minutes later, it stalled again at my community gate. When I contacted the owner, he told me that was "normal" behavior for Amazon vans. That was the first time he ever mentioned what kind of van I had rented. I was shocked—and immediately concerned for my safety.
I was a 54-year-old woman traveling alone with my 11-year-old dog. If that van had stalled in the mountains, I could have been stranded at 10,000 feet or in 100+ degree temperatures going to and from. After thinking it through for about 20 minutes, I returned the van to where I picked it up from, placed the keys back in the lockbox, and informed the owner. He agreed to refund me, minus one day over text right after that. 
Despite returning it immediately and explaining the serious safety concerns, the owner denied that he had agreed to the refund, and RVshare backed him up.
UPDATE PON 09/23/2025: I had to dispute this with my bank. They took all of the information from me and RVshare along with the owner of the van denied, denied and denied. Finally it went to a 3rd party to make a decision and they decided in my favor. Don't give up. Fight for what is right and hopefully justice will prevail.

Very disappointed - found a rental trailer to use for guests for a week - rental about $700 - BUT THEY ADDED ON insurance I don't need to $674 

Travel trailer that the host is delivering, parking in my driveway and leaving it for a week?  Outrageous - DO NOT USE THIS SERVICE.

reviewTitle: This is a 3rd party site with a high…
reviewDescription: This is a 3rd party site with a high insurance price tag even when the RV is delivered. Their refund policy is terrible, no contract was ever sent however when we declined to use the site we were only partially refunded. GO DIRECTLY to owners/companies instead and skip this entire site.

The rv owner was a total pleasure to work with. Very responsive. Unit was exactly as advertised.  BUT, RV Share itself lacks any transparency. A, $125 a nite,  6 nite rental quickly became $1,662.46 on the credit card. As soon as you push the button agreeing to rent, you are also automatically agreeing to all ridiculous and exorbitant costs. There is no "choice". RV Share, in a separate email, was "unable to provide" a breakdown of "protection" costs. Several other hard -to -decipher and confusing texts and emails were generated.  Never, ever again. Do yourself a favor: read all the many negative reviews before choosing this company.

On two separate occasions we rented an RV through RVShare.  Both times, and on very short notice, we received notification that our reservation was cancelled.  No explanation was given until I was finally able to get up with the owners.  

Since renting RVs requires reservations at campsites, our entire vacation was in jeopardy.  We were lucky to find a gracious host the first time that had found themselves in that same position at one time.  They were lovely, but RVShare was of no assistance in finding a replacement RV.

The second and most recent time the host knew of mechanical issues a week before we received the cancellation and did not reach out.  

The other issue I have with RVShare is that since I am the one that plans vacations, and the RVShare account was already in my name, I was automatically the verified driver.  Nowhere did I see that the account holder was the primary driver.  

My husband was planning on doing all of the driving and when I called RVShare to find out why I had to pay an extra $13/day to have him verified as a driver the customer service representative, reluctantly waived this policy this one time.  

Within minutes, my husband was denied as a verified driver.  And while he didn't have any traffic or criminal violations he wasn't allowed to drive the RV.  I called RVShare and was told that they couldn't tell me over the phone or by email why he was denied, which makes no sense whatsoever.  They did say I would receive a notification from the company explaining the denial, which never came.  The customer service representative was anything but cordial and friendly.

Needless to say, we didn't even bother trying to find another RV through RVShare.  I found out about Outdoorsy and they have been a pleasure to work with.

I highly recommend avoiding RVShare.  There was no apology, on their part, and no offer to assist in finding another RV.  I noticed that Outdoorsy requires a vehicle maintenance check within 90 days of any rental.  And on top of that, their prices are MUCH less than RVShare.

It's not a company I would use again or recommend to anyone else.

Do not protect owners!  Rv Share allowed a renter to keep our unit for an unapproved extra night and then not collect on the funds that was owed to us. They also did not collect on any overage charges and or damages that were done as agreed upon they do not help the owners in any way shape or form They do not take any liability. They need to gather bank account information rather than credit card information that can be easily canceled before charges happen.

reviewTitle: Every time i called for help
reviewDescription: Every time i called for help, everyone went above and beyond  assistance. Especially Morgan

Horrible experience — RVshare insurance is a scam.
We rented an RV for Burning Man, paid $500 for cleaning and $1,200 for RVshare insurance, and returned it clean. The next day the owners accused us of ridicules  petty “damages” a missing pan we never used, a tiny chip and cushion scuff, even blaming an air freshener leaking that they had installed. We had photos and video proof, and the only issue we acknowledged upon return was a small scrape from tape used to keep the fridge shut (since it wasn’t working on pickup).
The real problem came from RVshare. A week later they hit us with $2,500 in charges without explanations for what were the damages. When we finally got through to the adjuster (who never contacted us first), we discovered the insurance is a total trap: the deductible is $1,000 for every rental, but for Burning Man it secretly doubles (which is not mentioned anywhere). We were charged in total a shocking $4,500 on top of 3900$ reservation. In practice, this means RVshare avoids paying anything and renters are expected to pay the entire bill ignoring the insurance completely.
So between dishonest owners and RVshare’s hidden policies, we paid thousands on top of cleaning and insurance that gave us zero protection.
Bottom line: RVshare enables shady owners, hides critical insurance details, and profits by leaving renters exposed. Don’t rent through them it’s not worth the risk.

No responden al teléfono
No siguen los casos
Cuando logras hablar con ellos son muy mal educados .
Mi opinión es muy mala

reviewTitle: DO NOT RENT YOUR CAMPER OUT HERE
reviewDescription:
DO NOT RENT YOUR CAMPER OUT HERE
Things were going great, I had responsible customers who took care of my RV. But then the nightmare happened. A guy brought it back in an absolute wreck. There was minor damages, but the cleaning was insane. There are optional fees on my RV (like cleaning the black and grey water tank out - the renter can do this themselves if they wish to avoid the fee) but the Renters can disregard overages!!! I did not know this until it happened to me and RVshare has no protection for these things like insane cleaning and other overages. They pretend they do, they even let you list overages (like excessive cleaning, generator time) on your listing, but there is no guarantee that you will be paid. The renter can do these overages and then just disregard them.

reviewTitle: How can you possibly trust a company…
reviewDescription: How can you possibly trust a company that won’t let you unsubscribe from their incessant advertising?  Obviously you cannot.

First, we rented a RV that they let them bail on us the day before the trip with zero compensation
Second after we scrambled with no help from RV share to find a second one. It was a complete piece of trash. I won’t even talk about the inside of the RV versus what the pictures look like because they had a battery in it that was completely unsafe and died on us multiple times including while driving. After showing them pictures that proved that this RV was completely unsafe for anyone to use. They refunded us no money and we have a Mechanic that will be testifying in court that this thing was completely unsafe to rent. Don’t put your family and loved ones at risk. There are a bunch of other companies that rent RVs do not go through these people

RV-Share kann ich nicht empfehlen. Hier werden Ehrlichkeit und Fairness nicht groß geschrieben. Wir hatten einen Camper gemietet bei dem ging eigentlich garnichts ausser fahren und schlafen und kochen. Die Zusatzangebote wie Fernsehen, Mikrowelle gingen garnicht. Der Kühlschrank ging ebenfalls nicht nur über Generator da ein Fehler auf der Elektrik war. Wir waren, da wir Naturcamper sind nicht auf konventionellen Campingplätzen somit waren wir unpluged. Als wir Zuhause waren kam eine Aufforderung weitere 1200,00 Dollar zu zahlen. Für die Generatornutzung. Komisch wir hatten den Generator allerdings nicht so lange an wie angegeben! Eine bodenlose Frechheit. Wir haben unsere kompletten Unterlagen eingereicht und einen Deposit gefordert, da die vereinbarten Leistungen nicht erbracht wurden. Trotz Zeugenaussagen, schriftliche Nachweise, datierte Bilder hält sich RV-Share zum Eigentümer und hält an der Forderung fest. Netterweise würden Sie uns ca. 200,00 Dollar bei der Nachzahlung erlassen. Am besten Finger weg von diesem Portal als Mieter von Camper! Lieber über Campervermietungen buchen und auf ein bissle Luxus verzichten (den wir ja trotzdem nicht hatten ;). Dies haben wir jetzt gelernt. Euch noch weiter schöne Campingurlaube.

reviewTitle: Jesse helped me she did great job
reviewDescription: Jesse helped me she did great job. Incredible work like no other. I’ve been struggling with other persons from Customer Service. They couldn’t help me yesterday but today Jessy was remarkable. She done the job and completed with great success. I’m telling you Jessy is the best for the entire RV share company God bless.

reviewTitle: I would not do it again!
reviewDescription: The RV was in very bad condition and did not reflect the description at all. Old tires, broken ceiling window, full & clogged black water tank, lots of personal clutter, every surface was sticky... just to name a few. What was most disappointing - RV Share did not even honour us with an answer to our complaint - so they obviously do not care about customer satisfaction at all. I would definitely not rent again with RV Share.

reviewTitle: RVshare rips you off by adding insurance you rejected
reviewDescription: While the RV owner was a pleasure to deal with, RVshare chose to rip me off with additional charges for insurance I did not ask for specifically. Literally rejected the coverage, got a confirmation email, and they charged me more anyway. They refuse to refund the overcharge.

reviewTitle: Large non-refundable service fee.  Renter BEWARE!
reviewDescription: I had rented an RV from RV Share for a week in June of 2025 and paid my money.  They contact me two weeks before my rental date and state the RV was double booked, so my reservation had to be changed.  I changed it to a date in September that I wasn't sure would work and I had a conflict so had to cancel the reservation.  They charged me $166.08 for a service fee that is non-refundable.  I don't remember reading about a non-refundable service fee.  Very disappointed.  DO NOT rent from these people.

I paid extra for RVshare’s cancellation protection insurance to cover us if anything went wrong. Despite this, RVshare canceled our RV rental at the last minute—after we had taken time off work and planned our entire family vacation. By the time they notified us, it was impossible to find another RV rental.

RVshare claimed the owner canceled the trip. I asked for compensation as promised in the cancellation coverage policy. Instead, I spent hours on hold with their overseas customer service, was bounced around, hung up on, and called back—only to be offered a pathetic $25 promo code for my next trip.

RVshare customer service is a nightmare: an endless phone tree loop, nearly impossible to reach a real person. This feels like an online company with almost no real staff. If you’re looking for a reliable RV rental, avoid RVshare—your trip, time, and money are too important to risk on a company that doesn’t honor its own cancellation policy.

reviewTitle: Used them for a third time in 2025
reviewDescription: Used them for a third time in 2025. One time each year for a week. There are offers for delivery, pickup and dump service if you dry camp for a week like us with 3 grandkids. The trailer we rented this year was horrible. The owner had there original new rv photos on the site. The unit was a filthy mess. Had no choice but to put up with it as we traveled from Oregon to California. RVShare does not monitor or control the shape of rv is in. Will not use them again.

I've rented my camper for 2 years on RV Share but I'm done. Most renters were great, left great reviews, never had problems.  However, I recently had a renter book 2 months in advance of Sturgis..which is typically booked out a year in advance but we had our camper unlisted for a while and out it back up.  He (Friday night) reported the a/c didn't work and I told him I'd have someone come out but it would probably be Monday.  Sunday evening he texts me thst the camper is absolutely filthy, theres dust on a shelf, the stove had grease on it, and the blinds are bent.  Blinds get bent, I'm not replacing them because 1 little blind is bent.  He's had it for 3 days after I cleaned it and suddenly it's filthy and it's my fault?  He demands I fix it or retrieve it so, seeing that this guy is going to be a nightmare, I opt for the latter and refund his unused days. I called RVSHARE and that was their suggestion. For a rental that should be non-refundable.  Now he's disputing having to pay at all.  For the 3 days he slept in it and got it dirty and for the (2) 4hr trips we had to make to deliver and pick it up.  I'm done.  RV Share already take most if what I'd make, I'm basically cleaning and delivering it for free and now this.  Don't rent on RV Share. It's not worth the trouble or the money you lose.

What a joke! The customer service and communication are terrible. Don't expect anyone at this company to help you with an rv rental. The process of reserving an RV is a joke. You pay and then sit there for 24 hours waiting for a response. After 24 hours the request just expires without any communication. I am surprised a crap company like this can stay in business. Don't waste your time to money. They also falsely advertise unlimited miles. Then tell you it is unlimited with a daily limit! lol! WTF?

Irresponsible and Unfair – Avoid RVshare
We rented a vehicle that broke down due to poor maintenance. Our family of six was left stranded and spent thousands of dollars arranging safe travel. RVshare promised reimbursement and then denied most of it after weeks of delays. Their process is disorganized and unfair. They keep your money, give you the runaround, and leave you with the bill. Not a trustworthy company.

reviewTitle: This company is corrupt and they are…
reviewDescription: This company is corrupt and they are crooks.  They scammed me of $187 fees they were supposed to pay out on my last rental.  They charged me $87.50 for a previous reservation I had to cancel several weeks in advance prior to the reservation date due to repairs that needed to be done.  The customer service is horrible.  Stay far away from this company.

I've rented from RV Share a couple times. The first was disappointing because the images absolutely did not reflect the vehicle we got. After the second time I will never use them again. After putting down the $2K deposit, the owner cancelled the ride about a month later. About a month before our trip, we wanted to get some more details about the RV, and found that the owner had cancelled the RV. He had never received any funds, and RV Share never sent us any warning other than a message in their app. No email, no text message, no call. NO REFUND (6 (SIX!) MONTHS after owner cancelled). The only warning about this was a message in their website messaging system, which most people are not going to check until closer to their trip. This just smells like extra scummy business practices to squeeze some extra cash out of their customers' funds they no doubt invest until they need to pay out. I recommend staying far away from companies like this.

reviewTitle: good luck getting anywhere with their 'customer service'
reviewDescription: As both a renter through RVshare and owner with our own RV listed, I am blown away with how terrible their customer service is. What else are they providing with their cut of the rental $$ if they can't find ANY WAY to be helpful????

I rented two RVs through RV share. In Northern Nevada RVs rents out for much more than the nightly rate I usually charge for Burning Man. There is special pricing on the bottom of listing for special events. I cancelled three reservations for Burning Man at the regular rate and RV Share charged me cancellation fees totaling almost a 1k. They act like they own my RVs. I told them to piss off and that I'm not paying them dookie and cancelled my listings. They are garbage if you're an owner of an RV renting on their site. Their customer service is BS. I wouldn't recommend renting through their site. Also, the customer service lady I spoke to was probably in Thailand and could barely speak English. Total non-sense.
Prompt handling of cancellation due to illness

Apparently there is some problem with information they need me to provide. I have no idea what that problem is. The FIRST I knew of this was when I received an email saying they CANCELLED MY BOOKING due to needing "information necessary to secure Insurance and Protection for your upcoming RV trip". I already signed into their site and provided that information, paid them in full for the rental, and signed up for and paid for insurance. 

So today they cancelled my booking
* Without notice. 
* Without telling me there was a problem

reviewTitle: Renters Beware – RVShare Sided with Dishonest Owner and Ignored All Proof
reviewDescription:
⭐ 1 Star – Deeply disappointed and disgusted by how RVShare handled our situation.

We rented an RV through RVShare and had one of the worst customer service experiences we’ve ever encountered. Despite being honest, respectful renters and providing overwhelming proof that a damage claim was false, RVShare chose to side with the owner without a proper investigation.

The RV owner, Dana Allen, falsely accused us of damaging her RV. My husband, who drove the vehicle, is a 20-year CDL truck driver with a flawless record no accidents, no damaged loads, currently driving hazardous materials. We take extreme pride in respecting other people’s property, and we returned the RV in the exact condition we received it.

Dana claimed she took photos of the RV prior to our rental, but when asked to produce them with time and date stamps (which any smartphone captures), she never did because those photos don’t exist. We requested this proof multiple times and got nothing. Still, RVShare charged us over $500 based on her unsubstantiated claim.

We went above and beyond:
	•	Submitted aerial photos showing how Dana parks her RV in a dangerously tight space (where damage clearly could’ve occurred)
	•	Provided context showing she admitted the RV was almost stuck in the mud before our trip
	•	Explained my husband’s impeccable driving history

None of it mattered. RVShare didn’t care. They didn’t defend the renter. They didn’t conduct a fair investigation. They just took the owner’s word.

📢 This is a warning to all renters:
RVShare does NOT protect you. If an owner lies, you’re at risk of losing hundreds of dollars no matter how much evidence you provide.

And to be very clear: Do NOT rent from Dana Allen. She is dishonest and untrustworthy. If you see her listings on RVShare or elsewhere, keep scrolling.

I will never use RVShare again and strongly advise others to look for more transparent, renter-friendly platforms. I’ve learned a costly lesson don’t let this happen to you.

Then in the afternoon I received an email saying the customer “didn’t verify info for insurance” and so they were canceling the trip. reviewTitle: Beware of Hidden Fees and Poor Customer Service
reviewDescription:
I attempted to rent an RV through RVshare, but after checkout I received an email informing me of several unexpected insurance fees—none of which were clearly disclosed upfront, and there was no way to opt out. I tried contacting customer service immediately, but no one answered. Days later, I finally spoke with someone who clearly had handled this type of complaint many times before. They walked me through the cancellation process—but still kept the “service fee” despite the misleading experience.

I’ve rented RVs, homes, and more over the years, and this was by far the strangest and least transparent experience I’ve had. I ended up canceling and losing money, but I felt compelled to leave a review so others are aware. There are better, more honest local companies out there—do yourself a favor and avoid RVshare.

reviewTitle: RV Share is an absolute nightmare of a…
reviewDescription:
RV Share is an absolute nightmare of a company—stay far away! I booked an RV on March 4th for a family trip, shelling out a ridiculous $1,600 for just 4 nights. I told them exactly where I was going, spent over a month studying the area and the RV, and made campground reservations to ensure everything was perfect. My family—kids included—was buzzing with excitement for what was supposed to be an unforgettable getaway.
Then, on April 8th—ONE DAY before I was set to pick up the RV on April 9th—I got a notification that flipped my world upside down: the RV I booked and paid for over 30 days ago was canceled. No warning, no reason, just a gut punch out of nowhere. I was in the middle of packing when it hit, and I couldn’t believe it. The day before pickup? Are you kidding me?
It gets worse. RV Share didn’t even have the decency to apologize. No “sorry,” no discount on another RV, no help finding an alternative—nothing. Just a smug, heartless “Screw you, too bad” vibe that left me stranded. I scrambled to contact them, but their customer service was a black hole: no answers, no support, just silence. They took my money and ran, leaving me with a canceled trip and a family to disappoint.
The emotional toll was brutal. My kids were crushed—they’d been counting down the days, and I had to look them in the eyes and explain why we weren’t going. All that planning, all that excitement—wasted. It’s not just upsetting; it’s infuriating. RV Share didn’t care one bit about screwing us over.
And here’s the real warning: this isn’t a fluke—it’s how they operate. Dig around online, and you’ll see this is common practice for RV Share. They lure you in with promises of a great trip, take your hard-earned cash, and then cancel at the last second with zero remorse. It’s predatory, plain and simple. People need to know what they’re signing up for with this sham of a company.
Do NOT book with RV Share. Save your money, your time, and your sanity. Go with a business that doesn’t treat customers like disposable trash. RV Share is a scam—they don’t deserve a single star, let alone your trust. Avoid them like the plague.

reviewTitle: Offering discounts and then not honoring them...
reviewDescription:
We booked an RV through RVShare months ago... two weeks before our trip, the owner cancelled our reservation. RVShare sent us 4 emails offering $60 off to book a new reservation through their site. It took a few hours to find something on such short notice that would work for us, and then the $60 off code would not work. 
When I called customer service they told me that the offer had expired... I had just received the most recent email less than 3 hours before making the reservation. They offered to send me a $25 off code... a far cry from $60 but at this point my hands are tied... I NEED an RV as I had already paid for the RV spot and made other arrangements based on having an RV on site for the event we are attending. Not to mention that there aren't any pet-friendly hotel rooms available in the area due to that event. They texted me the code... that one didn't work either... I called back... she sent me another code... which also didn't work. She then checked with her team and told me that at this time it appears promo codes are not working on the site at all. So, she said she would send me a $25 refund after the fact... I asked why I wouldn't get the $60 credit since that's the offer I was originally sent, now that we know codes aren't working. She said she would "try" but couldn't guarantee anything except the $25 off. 
This has been the most stressful and upsetting experience. It shouldn't be this difficult to honor something that you promised as a company. I didn't have a choice but to use them this time... but I won't use them again.

reviewTitle: This is a very fraudulent company
reviewDescription: This is a very fraudulent company. If anyone is seeking to rent an RV, do not go thru the process of renting from RVshare. This company breaks its own rules to make money. I rented a camper in OK in Sept 2024 for 4 days. First of all when I went to pick up this camper, the owner never showed up to do the out paperwork to go over any damage before I rented it. Camper was stored at a public storage facility. I had to call him to find out about how to get in the camper. I checked the lights before pulling it away and the entire right side lights didn't work. No brake lights , no turn signal. I drove it away anyway because I already paid for it and there was no time to find another one. I returned the camper on the return day 4 days later and still the owner was not there to receive the camper so no in paper work was completed like he was supposed to do per rvshare rules. 3 days later I received an email from rvshare stating that owner found nothing wrong with the camper and was releasing my security deposit of $500 back to me and that this transaction was completed. A couple of weeks later I receive an email from rvshare that the owner filed a claim for damages and I was asked by rvshare what kind of damage there was. I didn't know of any damage. The only thing was that my wife found a piece of plastic molding on the floor when we picked it up. That was it. On Oct 10th I received an email from rvshare that they were charging my credit card $398 for damages. I looked at my credit card account and there was a pending charge for that amount. I immediately called a Claims adjuster for rvshare and she explained that was for some sort of damages when i had the camper. I had to pay for insurance when I rented the camper. Company policy. She never did tell me what the $398 was for and I never did revieve any claims paperwork showing what I was paying $398 for. I told her that I was cleared by the owner on Sept 5th and my security deposit was returned. She told me that there could be more charges later. I asked for what and she couldn't tell me. Later that afternoon I received an email from her saying this account has been closed. I called my credit card company and had my card locked and closed in case anymore fraudulent transactions occurred. I will never ever deal with this company again.

reviewDescription: Disgusting, AVOID at all costs as a renter. RV Share have perfected being an excellent scamming tool for RV owners to be able to fix up their broken, falling apart RV's by placing them in the hand of customers and waiting for something to (inevitably) go wrong so they can rinse them for RV repairs. AVOID at all costs, they ignore customers and don't look at any evidence. The RV we rented was run down, taped and zip-tied together, the owner wasn't present and provided old zoomed out photos to RV Share, within minutes of being on the freeway after collection the awning dangerously swung off, we pulled over to find it had only been propped on with one rusted threadless screw, had a number of holes where it had been reattached previously. We spoke to the owner immediately on the platform who was disinterested and told us to just 'screw it back on'. Upon return, we were later charged our entire deposit plus another $600 that they just took from my card without informing me. We sent time stamped photos as they requested, gave the full description of what happened showing all of the issues that the owner didn't disclose (with location and time stamps from collection) as well as the evidence of what had happened when we set off showing all of the holes where it had been screwed back on again previously. They didn't even look at any of it, they will take your money without informing you. Their 'customer service' is non-existaent and Kyla is one of the rudest people I have ever encountered. They did not send a breakdown of what was being claimed for, they did not send any documentation of any of it and just took money from my card. STAY AWAY FROM THIS SCAM AGENCY.

reviewDescription:
DO NOT RENT FROM RV SHARE. We were VERY mislead by the RVshare listing that we rented. The tires that were on the back of the RV, were not only BALD - two of them completely blew (and I mean SHREDDED) 1 hour into our trip. We were left on the side of the highway (US 16 - which is a minimum MPH of 70 with tractor trailers blowing by every 2-3 minutes), from 10:45 p.m. until 5:45 the next morning. WE had to pay out of pocket, $1600 to have the two tires replaced that should have been replaced prior to our trip, and have not been reimbursed by either the owner or RV Share. Not only was our safety completely jeopardized, but we were not able to get ahold of the "24/7" roadside assistance that RV share claims to have. After calling the number on their website, 6 times, waiting on a hold tone line for 30 minutes EACH and every time, the call would end and hang up on us. THIS IS FALSE ADVERTISEMENT AND FRAUDULENT. They left PAYING customers in an extremely dangerous and potentially fatal situation, and they have STILL not made this right. We spent $1560 on the RV, plus $1600 on the two tires that should have been replaced PRIOR to our trip. We lost an entire day spent sitting along the side of a dangerous highway, while waiting for someone to come and fix our tire.  

Also, this RV was overall a complete disappointment. The advertisement claims it sleeps 8, however the owners screwed in the table settee that is supposed to fold down into a bed, (and FAILED TO LET US KNOW prior) rendering that space completely useless. This is false advertisement. On TOP of that, the entire side bump out motor (that had "two" sleeping areas) did not even function. We had to manually push the entire slider out, so that our space was usable for sleep. It took all members of our group, to open the slider, and all of us to close the slider from the outside in on our final day. The bathrooms were dirty and looked like they had not been cleaned in years - dust and dirt and general grime. The master bathroom overhead window/vent was completely falling apart and unable to be closed, letting in bugs constantly. The doors on the master bedroom didn't even close, because they were off the rails. Not only has RV Share not provided us with a reimbursement for the money and fees that we paid for out of pocket, they don't respond to emails, they provide you with a claims specialist that does not help either and says that they are surprised that someone from their team has not yet reached out, and then goes MIA. RV Share is a complete waste of your time. You are going to be sadly mislead on any RV that you rent. This is our second time, and LAST time renting from RVShare, as BOTH experiences were completely misleading and there is no quality assurance on the RVs they are comfortable and COMPLIANT renting to paying customer.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hello Cassy, we apologize for the issues you have faced. Please reach out to us at 1-888-482-0234.

reviewTitle: We rented a broken RV and were charged to fix what was broken
reviewDescription:
We rented an RV and faced numerous issues, yet were unfairly charged $1,387 for damages we did not cause even after paying $347 in insurance.
	
	Booking & Pickup: On April 15, 2024, we contacted Nedra Woods, the RV owner, to rent the RV for 3 days. We negotiated early pickup and late drop-off for $100 each, and finalized the booking on April 19. On May 1, the owner informed us that the RV had a damaged door that couldn't be fixed in time. The door could NOT be closed from the INSIDE. She offered a $200 discount, which we accepted due to our travel plans with friends. Pics attached.
	
	Condition of RV: Upon pickup, the RV was dirty and had several damages. We documented the dirt in the bedroom and on the couch, and the exterior damages. At the campground, we found the awning inoperable due to duct tape. Despite owner's assurance that the awning was functional (!), the tape prevented it from moving and removing the tape revealed the broken frame, making it unsafe in the wind. We never left it unattended or open overnight contrary to the baseless claims from the owner. Pics attached.
	
	Safety Hazards: The rear left luggage compartment couldn't be locked and opened while we were on the road, requiring us to tape it shut. The exterior light and some power sockets were non-functional, leaving us reliant on the awning's LED lights. Pics attached.
	
	Incident with Rear Window: While parked, my sister tried to open the rear window, which immediately shattered without force. We cleaned the area and taped the shades to secure the RV, storing the window frames in a luggage compartment. Pics and video attached show that it couldn't have been hit by a tree, as owner baselessly claimed.
	
	Despite comprehensive evidence (photos, videos, and comments), RVShare unfairly charged us $1,387. This charge is unjustified as we did not cause the damages listed. We demand a fair review and resolution of this matter with full refund for this upsetting, aggravating and frustrating experience.
reviewCompanyResponse: Carlos, you should be receiving an email shortly regarding the further investigation of this issue. Thank you for your patience.

Just tell us about this silly, significant extra fee ahead of time! Hiding it shows incredibly poor integrity, and feels gross. Do better.

We require insurance for stationary rentals because accidents still can happen when the RV doesn't move and RVs are expensive assets. Our protection plans will cover the unit incase of theft, vandalism, fire, flood, lightning, explosions, earthquakes, storms, hail, acts of God, etc. We do show a total breakdown of the costs at the time of booking as well. reviewDescription: Buyer be aware.  The insurance that RVshare forces you to purchase is a scam and in some states illegal. I do not need road side which they make you purchase. To top that they cover windshield repair along other things that a trailer does not even have.  It is very clear that after you have already paid the fee that you cannot get out of the contract now without loosing the fee amount.  I total scam.  Other rental company’s do not do this and the rentals are less money.  It is strongly suggested to not use RVshare unless you enjoy being forced to paid insurance for something that does not exist. A good example is a broken windshield.  FYI a trailer does not have a windshield and if the trailer is delivered why would RVshare require me to have roadside insurance.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Robert, we're sorry to hear about your experience. Every RV rental is required to secure RVshare Insurance and Protection in case anything accidental happens while the RV is on a trip, there are exceptions to this rule in some cases. This also includes trips where the RV might be delivered or stationary for the duration of the trip. Even if the RV is delivered or stationary, damages may still occur, examples include fire, acts of nature like storms and falling trees, etc. 

Coverage during the rental period will vary depending on the package is selected during checkout. For more information on what the package includes visit the Insurance and Protection page at https://rvshare.com/insurance

reviewTitle: I have rented my RV on RVshare for…
reviewDescription: I have rented my RV on RVshare for years.  Not happy with changes that the company is making because they don’t protect the renter.  Renting an RV is a lot of work and takes a lot of time keeping your rig road ready.  They have a very strict cancelation policy and they have loop holes that allow renters to bypass the cancelation policy and that puts RV owners in a bad spot. I live in the mountains and have to setup my RV prior to renter and tear it down and not store anything in it during downtime. With this policy loop hole I have lost out on lots of my time when they fail to verify insurance till the last minute. This loop hole should be closed so that Rv owners time are protected. I have delisted by RV with them and will be using other sites.  I enjoy renting my RV allowing others to experience RV life and meeting new people. It’s unfortunate that it has come to this.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hi Dominic, we're sorry to hear about your experience. The information necessary was not completed to secure The RVshare Protection Plan, so your reservation was canceled. Thank you so much Ross.

NEIL (B) with the CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE TEAM went OVER & BEYOND in a situation that had us in a bind with our damaged rv. There's still GREAT GENUINELY caring people out here and we are glad to partner with a company like RVSHARE that employs gentlemen like NEIL (B). GREAT JOB & THANK YOU SO MUCH.

This was a horrible experience. The door broke early on during the trip (not our fault). The RV Share representative had mentioned that there was a problem with the door getting stuck when we went to rent it, but we did not realize exactly what he meant. We thought that he meant that it was just a little hard to open. However, we soon realized that when that door got stuck, it became IMPOSSIBLE to open! We had to climb in and out of the passenger door the entire time, which was very difficult. Roadside Assistance refused to help us, declaring that it was not an emergency. Second, it was supposed to sleep 10, but it truly only slept about 6, and we had rented it precisely because it slept 10. We NEEDED it to sleep 10 people, so that was extremely deceptive. When we confronted the RV share representative with this fact, he claimed that these upright recliners could sleep 2. These recliners only reclined partially rather than fully flat, so there is no way in which most people could actually sleep in such an upright position. Also, there is no way that it even comfortably slept even 8 adults. Maybe it could comfortably sleep 6 adults, but even that would be stretching it. The first night, we couldn't even get the table/bed to work properly, so it only slept about 5. Third, the insurance and fees were a total racket - way expensive! Fourth, the bathroom stunk. The backup camera is not to be trusted when backing these things up. My husband accidentally hit something, and it made a very small dent in the back, and so we lost our entire damage deposit.  We will never rent from them again!
reviewCompanyResponse: Hi Shawn, we apologize for this experience you had with the RVshare owner. We can certainly look into this further. Please reach out to us at 1-888-482-0234.

reviewTitle: Paid and Website is unable to complete the Reservation
reviewDescription:
I tried to rent a RV from them, filled out all the info, and paid the money via credit card.  I got back a few minutes latter an email that my ID check failed.  I emailed and called for help, but all they could say is keep trying.  Well the webpage and links they sent to verify kept showing up as a blank screen.  I tried multiple browsers and PC's with no luck.  I used Chrome to see there were HTTP 500 Internal Server errors and sent screen shots, but no one helped to seemed to contact their own IT department.

It makes me feel they created this website to take your money and make you so frustrated that you cancel the reservation yourself and then loose a portion of payment.

Update: 30-Dec-2023.  After conversations with a manager on the phone, it was clear there was no simple solution on getting my data uploaded for verification.  I asked for a 100% refund and the team responsible for the reservation was kind enough to refund to my CC 100% of the charges within a business day.  Thank you for helping to fix the situation.

reviewTitle: DO NOT rent from RV Share!

Save yourself unnecessary stress and heartache - 5 days before my trip and amid the holidays they allowed the owner to cancel my booking and then penalize me, the customer whose money has been committed for months. Now days before I have purchased flights and activities I have to break the news to my kids that we are not able to have the vacation we planned for months during the holiday season!
The rep told me to contact potential owners to rebook. What utter nonsense, what is the point of their third-party fees if they do nothing to support their impacted customers.
The staff lacks empathy, they are unreliable, unempathetic and have deplorable customer service. 
I highly suggest working with independent companies who care about their customers than this useless service.
After days of stress and escalation the day before my scheduled trip, a rep finally contacted me to acknowledge my complaint and offer me 5% off a future booking. After such a negative experience why would I ever give them another chance?

I just finished my trip and successfully acquired another RV from TxRVAdventures I concur that RV Share has the most deplorable customer service and is not worth the stress, they offer nothing and you can get so much more from direct RV companies. I highly recommend you do your research and if you have to pay a little more to get value for your money and have a stress-free booking explore booking with direct companies, it is a night and day experience.

I had been working on this for a little bit and had spoken with  representative who had said that another department would email me and to take screenshots of the current listing, then re-enter the information once the other department notified me.  Well, we had a person that wanted to rent the RV, but couldn't because of this dilemma. I called again today and got Bryan. I explained my situation and he took care of the issue while I waited on the phone.  I'm so happy that Bryan stepped in!!!  And, we didn't lose our renter!  Thank you again Bryan!!

reviewTitle: Don't trust RVShare and their insurance.
reviewDescription: A renter destroyed so much of my trailer and RVShare did nothing to help me recoup the repair costs.  We kept the deductible, but they did nothing to help recoup the other $1000 in damages.  They told us tough luck.  The insurance they sold to the renters didn't cover the first $1500 (deductible), but informed me that it was fully covered.  Now I am out $1000 because the renter backed my trailer into some trees, had dogs tear up the inside, and busted up the outside bumper grill.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hello Courtney, we're so sorry to hear about the damage to your RV. We recommend reaching out to our Damages team for further information on your insurance claim. You can reach us 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

reviewTitle: The website is easy to use and has all…

Other than that we had no big issues, and Mike was great! We very much appreciated being able to rent his RV.
Great communication from start to finish.
It was very simple, there were some pricing issues but it was worked out between me and the owner. I’d definitely book again.

Awesome experience from beginning to end. We will be renting from RVshare again!

We had a good experience but there were a few items (such as no taillights) that could have caused an issue for us and damage to the owners vehicle.
Also some additional resources for first time renters would be great - things like not being able to use certain appliances when the vehicle is in motion, local dump stations, and the type of power connections needed to hook away from a RV park would all have been helpful - our owner didn't cover these items but I couldn't expect him to know our level of expertise so not his fault!

reviewTitle: The owners of the RV were great
reviewDescription: The owners of the RV were great! They were responsive, kind and professional. They made  the rental very easy, and my family enjoyed the unit. Would recommend them and RV Share to anyone looking to rent a unit.

I added similar comments in the owners review, not realizing I could provide a separate review for my experience with RVshare. I think if you look at the correspondence I had with your associates in an effort to try and obtain a refund of the money I had paid before learning the RV was not suitable to take on the road, you will have all the information you need. The bottom line is, your insurance clause is unacceptable for someone who is not able to use the RV for what it was intended for. Just because it sat in my driveway for a couple of hours before we noticed carbon monoxide was leaking in, should not constitute me not receiving a full refund of the insurance amount. 
I will not use, nor refer anyone to RVshare.

Edited on 12/15/2023: 
After posting the review above, RVshare contacted me and conducted another review of my case and completed the reimbursement of the remaining amount. I am thankful they chose to reconsider the events impacting my reimbursement request. For this, I have changed my overall rating to 3 stars.

reviewTitle: I really enjoyed my first rental but…
reviewDescription: I really enjoyed my first rental experience. I did have one issue with the RV share group and that is the "fees and costs". I had to pay 2 times the cost of renting the R.V. to insure it and to have RVshare be the intermediary for the rental. When I returned the rental there was an expected over mileage charge that I was expecting to pay. The biggest thing that bothered me was RVshare charged me a fee for my excessive daily mileage as if RVshare did anything that the over mileage fee needs to cover such as maintenance and repairs. I guess what my issue is the person renting the rig to me got about 39% of the bill while RVshare group made 61%. The rig owner  took all of the risk and had to pay for the extra maintenance the mileage created.

I understand insurance of course, but the service fees added up fast, the $78 per night RV added up to over $500 for 2 nights, by the time I paid the service fee, taxes and insurance. Overall it was a good experience though.

reviewTitle: The rentals were good but the fees were…
