reviewTitle: Choosing the RV was the easy bit
reviewDescription: Choosing the RV was the easy bit. Paying for the rental and insurance with my UK international credit card was more difficult. Several attempts were made. Initially successful, the CC company AI decided that there was fraudulent activity and cancelled my card. I was informed by a phone call at 02.30 am in the middle of the dessert! Luckily the insurance payment had been accepted by another card- but we didn’t know that at that point. Not the best start! We had a fantastic time touring in Nevada- Lake Mead, Valley of Fire, Red Rocks and in Arizona- Willow Beach, Lake Havasu, Kingman, Grand Canyon, Lake Havasu Cattail Park and Route 66. The support from the RV staff and the owners was great. We were able to take our time and really get the feeling of the places we visited. We never rushed. Places we missed are on a list for the next time we visit the USA. We have lifetime memories!

My experience was very good. I WISH IT WAS EASIER to locate someone locally..I had a problem with the RV's that I really liked being a couple of states away! I will use  you again and I am recommending you to my friends!

I booked an RV through RVShare because I needed it for an event we were going to sponsor in six months.  After two days, I realized that I was going to need to sponsor the same even in a different state as opposed to the one I initially planned for.  I was going to cancel the reservation and re-book for another location; however, RVShare charged me a $69 "RVShare service fee."  I know how much it cost to acquire a new customer like me, and I know how much it cost to keep a potential good customer.  Hint: It's cheaper to keep a new customer, something that RVShare is oblivious to.  They got my $69, but I estimated my need for this kind of service over the next twenty years and I'm probably one of many who will blacklist this company forever.

We rented this RV in good faith that it was in working condition.  We drove 98 miles to our first destination.  Once we parked the carbon monoxide monitor went off immediately.  Also, the water hose was bent at the connector and water went everywhere on the outside.  We would have had to purchase a new water hose in order to use for the three days we had rented.  Also, the sewer connector did not have fitting to connect to the outside tank.  
The ac in the front part of the unit does not work on the passenger side well.
We could have dealt with other items, however, we did not feel comfortable sleeping with the carbon monoxide possible problem, so we turned around and drove home with windows down on unit to ensure our wellbeing.
We returned the unit the next morning.  No one was at the location, so we were informed to leave keys in box and park which we did.  I have video and pictures of all.
We requested at least a partial refund because I had paid for nights in RV parks we didn't get to use.  Instead Scott charged against yhe cc he had on file an additional charge because we did not file up with gas.
I have talked with an attorney and plan to take this to small claims court.
Sincerely
Charlotte Reed

reviewCompanyResponse: Hi John, the way we handle security deposits is by placing a "hold" on your account for the amount of the security deposit. When that happens, your bank creates a pending transaction but never actually processes that payment. Because of that you were never actually charged that money, you won’t see a refund in your account. Instead, that original pending transaction will either say that it was voided, or it will have disappeared altogether. If you have any further questions, you can reach us 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

reviewTitle: I rented a motorhome from RVShare
reviewDescription: I rented a motorhome from RVShare.  Price was decent, but the insurance was really high even for the highest deductible price.  Insuring it with my own insurance company wasn't an option.  My insurance company would have added the week's rental to my policy for less than $20 rather than the almost $500 insurance through RVshare.  I also paid a $500 security deposit.  The motorhome owner seemed very nice.  The motorhome was pretty nice.  My trip was fine except when I got to my destination, my brother noticed there was a flat tire on the inside dual tire.  He also said the other tires were in horrible condition from sun and weather exposure.  He was upset at the owner for sending me with them in that condition.  I wasn't used to driving something tall and I brushed up against tree branches.  That damaged the awning and paint finish.  My brother said for the awning to fall apart the way it did, it like the tires had to have been already in horrible condition due to sun and weather damage.  The 24 hour roadside service person agreed with his conclusion.  I spent a half a day in the tire shop.  I returned the RV.  The seemingly nice owner fraudulently charged me with generator hour overage which I absolutely did not use it more than a couple hours.  I disputed the overages.  RVShare withdrew the $1000 (which added to the $500 security deposit is the $1500 deductible) plus the $200+ overages from my bank account without notice as soon as damage and overages we're reported and before the dispute or insurance adjustment even had a chance to get started.  It is impossible to talk to anyone at RV share who is located in the USA.  Their international customer service people are helpful before the trip, but can't do anything for you afterward.  I contested the withdrawals and the overdraft charges with VISA.  VISA ruled in my favor and refunded my money, but now I can't get a hold of anyone to solve payment of the damages.  RVShare has non-existent customer service after the sale, bad business practices of withdrawing money from bank accounts prematurely, and absolutely no communication.

We also took out the insurance and ended up needing it for an incident, and that has been resolved very quickly and easily, response has been great.

The one suggestion I would make is to require owners to accurately list, in detail, what equipment is included on the rig.  General comments such as "outfitted kitchen" or "outdoor gear" are not helpful.  One of our recent rentals had a few pots and pans, no plates cups or cutlery, though the description implied the inclusion.  We had to get paper plates, plastic forks, etc.  Our most recent rental included a fully itemized list organized by on board cupboards and basement storage bins.  We referred to this list several times in search of basement items; in one case a first aid kit.  We looked at the on board itemization and found the kit immediately. A huge help in prepping for the trip.

reviewTitle: RVShare and Digusure are scammer companies
reviewDescription:
Dear RVShare and Digisure, with reference to my last review «Let's face the truth» to which you didn't even bother to give a response. I would like to evidence to everyone what has happened in the last two weeks …

Digisure representative, showed us (via email) a photo of a damaged roof of one RV which we don't know whether or not it portrayed the RV rented by us, without even a date stamped on the photo to at least understand if the photo had been taken in the period of our rental. And with this “fake photo” he took 1,500 USD from our credit card and ran away…. Together with RVShare. Company of clowns !!! 
 
To ensure that everyone knows about it, I am publishing the article 11 of the RVShare contract with which they steal money from renters .

According to the Terms of Service, Paragraph 11, Damage to RVs; Renter Responsibilities.  https: rvshare.com ... terms-of-service
 
  • Renters will be held liable for any damage that cannot be proven to have existed prior to  the rental period.  

I don't know if you have understood!!! With the article above, RVShare and Digisure can charge anything to the renters... as they did to us !!! So dear gentlemen, future customers, if you want to use RVShare, when you pick up the RV plese photograph everything (front, back, roof , wheels, underbody, internal and external punctures, BBQ, generator, all the staff .... everything !!!) and when you return the RV, ask to the Owner to check the overall condition of the RV in front of you and pretend he sign a declaration. Otherwise, you’ll go home and few hours later RVShare and Digisure will charge on your card an amount to cover a «fake damage» that you never caused!!! 

RVShare, Digisure: you are clowns and scammer !!! 

Give us back the money you stole from us (ref. Accident RVSUS3233914). We sent you all the evidence including photos with stamped dates showing that the roof of the RV was not damaged!!!

If this is your first time, using RV share, I would think of it as one of those Bnb apps but for RVs. You are renting a private owners of the RV, with RVshare as the intermediary (software service). 
You can pick up your RV/trailer or pay for delivery.
We opted to have ours delivered to our campsite.
If this is your first time, renting you sure to choose your spot and rent it for the whole time that you want it. Be sure you are there when the owner arrives to set up. They will explain to you all the ins and outs of operating the RV especially if you have a delivery. Take full advantage of this time to ask all your questions to the owner.
The whole process was delightful and the owner showed up right on time. They set up the RV just what we wanted it. we definitely had the best time and will definitely be using RV share again.

reviewDescription:
Great experience, just one flaw:
Overall I was very happy with my experience.  The whole process went smoothly.
One thing I did not understand was the pricing-policy:
After a 10 nights rental period the owner of the trailer and me tried to extend the drop-off-time from 09:00 AM to 03:00 PM for my convenience. Unluckily this resulted in an additional fee of more than 200 USD!! This is absolutely ridiculous! The rental period was about to end at the same day, literally. So how does RVShare dare to assume that it would be another night? 
Last but not least it turned out that extending from 09:00 AM to noon results in 1 Cent additional fee. This is absolutely not understandable to me. I paid a lot of money and I'm sure that RVshare gets a generous cut on every rental agreement, so this pricing policy is not customer-friendly at all!

reviewDescription: Communication was fast, as well as helping figure out the add drivers.  Only thing is, we had 2 drivers never approved and they charged us anyway. I would go through RVshare again in the future.

The camper we rented ended up breaking down. We called RV share and they gave us the insurance number. The insurance carrier was a joke. We sat on the side of the highway ( very busy and very dangerous) for three hours all while on the phone with an agent that had no idea what the hell she was doing. Left me on hold upwards of 20 to 30 minutes at a time. She almost towed the camper to a place that restores campers not fix the engine. I had to do my own research to find the correct place that would service the camper. Then I called RV share to find out how they were going to help. Your company was no help. Why the hell would we pay for another camper to rent when we just spent all our money on the one that broke down. Any time I spoke to a representative it just irritated me even more. We ended up having to rent an SUV to get us home. The owner of the camper Daniel was amazing. He communicated with us through the whole ordeal and told us not to worry about anything.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hi Alyssa, we're sorry to hear about the troubles you faced during your RV rental. reviewTitle: The Air B n B of RVs
reviewDescription: Our first RV Share experience went very smooth. We choose RV share because we want to eventually buy our own, but wanted to try different sizes and options to see what would work best for us.    We picked out our RV, contacted the owner, (who was awesome, friendly, kind, informative, besides that the RV was spotless and had everything you needed to tow)  we got approval to rent.  Next we got the insurance coverage for the RV through RV Share.  Everything is very simple. We feel the prices were fair, for the rental and the insurance.   The only part I did not like was after we booked the RV I was getting emails from other RV owners trying to get me to rent theirs.  I understand what they are doing, but after our trip was booked and we made a commitment. I did not feel it was necessary to be bothered by other owners.  I think that during the process of renting, having owners with similar RVs would be quite helpful.   We can't wait to go again.  Thank you!!  I tell everyone this company is the Air B n B of Rv's!!

reviewTitle: Always on time and very c lean units
reviewDescription: Always on time and very c lean units

here is a copy of my dispute form, trip was horrible, RVShare did not help much. We will use another service moving forward.
RV rental condition was not "as advertised" in many ways, plus a few other unacceptable issues.
1. Expired Inspection, which is both unsafe and illegal. We noticed this after departure. If we were to be pulled over, or worse in an accident caused by neglected repairs this could have been very bad. (Unacceptable, see attached pic "Expired Inspect.jpg")
2. Upon flushing the toilet and/or using the sink water the stench that filled the RV was unbearable. This made the bathroom toilet, shower, sink and kitchen sink unusable for the duration of the trip. (Unacceptable)
3. We had rain forecasted during our days at camp so the awning feature advertised was very very important in our decision to choose this particular RV. Awning was broken off so we were left with out the option for shelter to sit outside during the rain. (Not "as advertised", see attached pics "No_Awning1.jpg", "No_Awning2.jpg" and "No_Awning3.jpg")
4. We chose not to sign-off on the Departure Form, per the advise given to us by customer service upon calling RVShare to discuss our situation after we headed out on our trip. As you will see in attached pics, the images sent by owner on the departure form do not accurately represent the RV we drove away with (Awning is shown intact, as well as some of the pics were direct copies from the initial rental posting, they were not current or taken the day of departure, as it should have been). See attached screenshots from the departure form, "DepartureForm1.jpg" and "DepartureForm2.jpg".
5. Lastly in general we feel the RV advertisement was very misleading and we did not receive what we expected we were going to get. We rented with RVShare for a 8 days trip in Arizona.
2 days before the rental, while we were already on holiday, unfortunately the owner tells us that the RV rented is not available!!!
RVshare did not help us at all in this emergency situation since communication with them was extremely hard and multiple people were involved and never was there appropriate follow-up.
So we had to find another RV through the RVshare circuit on our own and since it was July 28th it wasn't easy at all!!!! We have spent the whole two days in Las Vegas looking for a new RV without any support by RVshare!!! 
 The cost of the new RV, although equivalent to the previous one, was 50% higher!!!

Once the RV was returned, the Owner of the RV was very ambiguous and did not want in any way, despite our pressing requests, to check the condition of the RV. After 2 days RVShare and Digisure (the insurance company linked to RVShare) without any notice withdrew $1,500 from our credit card notifying us of a claim to the roof of the RV!!!. We have had absolutely NO ACCIDENTS!!!!
Unfortunately, we could not prove it because we did not shoot any pictures to the roof of the RV when we rented it. The roof of the RV is 13.5 feet above the ground!!!
We asked RVshare to send us photos of the roof to prove that it was intact before the rental but they were never provided.
Despite numerous calls and e-mails to the references above, nothing has happened to solve the said issue.
This is clearly a fraud!!! 

Referring back to the claim RVSUS3233914, we demand reimbursement for the amount they charged us without referring to us first.

This was a really awful experience!!! Absolutely avoid RVshare!!!

reviewCompanyResponse: Hi Eileen, the way we handle security deposits is by placing a "hold" on your account for the amount of the security deposit. When that happens, your bank creates a pending transaction but never actually processes that payment. Because of that you were never actually charged that money, you won’t see a refund in your account. Instead, that original pending transaction will either say that it was voided, or it will have disappeared altogether. If you have any further questions, you can reach us 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.
